title: Early education and childcare practitioners
description: Assist a preschool, elementary, middle, or secondary school teacher with instructional duties. Serve in a position for which a teacher has primary responsibility for the design and implementation of educational programs and services. Assist a preschool, elementary, middle, or secondary school teacher to provide academic, social, or life skills to students who have learning, emotional, or physical disabilities. Serve in a position for which a teacher has primary responsibility for the design and implementation of educational programs and services. All teaching assistants not listed separately. (Based on 8 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Support children's emotional and social development, encouraging understanding of others and positive self-concepts.
Model appropriate social behaviors and encourage concern for others to cultivate development of interpersonal relationships and communication skills.
Supervise and assist with homework and help children with school work.
Assign appropriate chores and praise targeted behaviors to encourage self-control, self-confidence, and responsibility.
Transport children to schools, social outings, and medical appointments.
Instruct and assist children in the development of health and personal habits, such as eating, resting, and toilet behavior.
Teach and perform age-appropriate activities, such as reading, arts and crafts, and social skills to encourage children's intellectual development.
Maintain a safe play environment, remove hazards, and develop appropriate rules for children's safety.
Communicate with children's parents or guardians about daily activities, behaviors, and related issues to ensure cooperation.
Observe children's behavior for irregularities, take temperatures, and administer first aid or CPR when required.
Organize and supervise recreational activities and outings, such as games and field trips, to promote physical, mental, and social development.
Prepare and serve nutritionally balanced meals and snacks for children and help in food preparation.
Create developmentally appropriate lesson plans and prepare lesson materials for children's learning activities.
Keep records of individual children, including observations, activities, meals served, and medications administered.
Enforce administration policies and rules governing students while managing behavior and discipline.
Provide assistance to students with disabilities and instruct them in daily living skills such as hygiene and safety.
Instruct and monitor students in the use and care of classroom equipment and materials, preventing injuries and damage.
Organize and store toys and learning materials to ensure order in activity areas for children.
Discuss assigned duties with classroom teachers to coordinate instructional efforts and support educational goals.
Enforce safety rules and regulations.
